meta.title: Launch Your SaaS in Hours, Not Months | SassyPack
meta.description: Stop wasting months on boilerplate. Launch your SaaS in 2-4 hours with SassyPack. Pre-built auth, payments, dashboards, and APIs included.

content.h1: Launch Your SaaS in Hours With SassyPack

hero.problem: Building SaaS from scratch takes 3-6 months. Authentication alone takes weeks. Payment integration is a nightmare. You're stuck building infrastructure instead of features that make money.

whyHard.heading: Why Launching SaaS Takes So Long
points: Authentication setup: 2-3 weeks (JWT, sessions, password reset, email verification)
Payment integration: 2-4 weeks (Stripe docs, webhooks, subscription logic, failed payments)
Admin dashboards: 2-3 weeks (charts, tables, user management, analytics)
API structure: 1-2 weeks (routes, middleware, validation, error handling)
Deployment setup: 1 week (configs, CI/CD, environment variables, monitoring)

solution.heading: The SassyPack Solution
solution.description: All the infrastructure built and tested. Install, customize your branding, and launch.
benefits: 2-4 hours from install to production (vs 3-6 months custom)
All features pre-built and tested in production
Focus on your unique value, not reinventing auth
Launch faster than your competitors

meta.title: Skip Authentication Setup | Pre-Built Auth in SassyPack
meta.description: Stop wasting weeks on auth. JWT authentication, password reset, email verification, and RBAC built into SassyPack. Production-ready in hours.

hero.problem: Authentication takes 2-3 weeks to build properly. JWT tokens, refresh logic, password hashing, email verification, session management, security audits. One mistake and your users are at risk.

whyHard.heading: Why Authentication Is So Complex
points: JWT token generation and validation logic
Refresh token rotation to prevent hijacking
Secure password hashing with bcrypt or argon2
Email verification flows with expiring tokens
Password reset with secure token generation
Session management and automatic logout
Role-based access control (RBAC) for different user types
Rate limiting to prevent brute force attacks
CORS and security headers configuration
Testing authentication flows end-to-end

solution.heading: The SassyPack Solution
solution.description: Production-grade authentication built, tested, and secured.
benefits: JWT authentication with automatic token refresh
Email verification and password reset flows included
Role-based permissions (admin, user, team member)
Security best practices: rate limiting, CORS, helmet.js
Works with both Nextjs and Next.js stacks

meta.title: Skip Payment Integration Headaches | Stripe Built Into SassyPack
meta.description: Stripe integration with webhooks, subscriptions, and failed payment handling built-in. Accept payments in hours, not weeks.

content.h1: Avoid Payment Integration Headaches With SassyPack

hero.problem: Stripe documentation is 1000+ pages. Webhooks fail silently. Subscription logic is complex. Failed payments need recovery flows. Testing in production is scary. Most founders spend 2-4 weeks on payments.

whyHard.heading: Why Payment Integration Is So Hard
points: Stripe Checkout integration with session management
Webhook endpoints that verify signatures correctly
Subscription lifecycle events (created, updated, canceled, failed)
Failed payment retry logic and customer notifications
Invoice generation and automated email delivery
Customer portal for subscription management
Handling edge cases: refunds, partial payments, upgrades
Syncing payment status with your database
Testing webhooks without breaking production
PCI compliance and security requirements

solution.heading: The SassyPack Solution
solution.description: Stripe integration with all the hard parts solved.
benefits: Stripe Checkout integration ready to use
Webhook handlers for all subscription events
Automatic failed payment recovery
Customer portal for self-service management
Works with both Nextjs and Next.js

meta.title: Production-Ready SaaS Template | Secure, Scalable, Fast
meta.description: Launch with confidence. Security, scalability, monitoring, and best practices built into SassyPack. Production-ready from day one.

hero.problem: Most starter kits are demos, not production code. They lack security, scalability, error handling, monitoring, and deployment configs. You end up rewriting everything before launch.

whyHard.heading: What Production-Ready Means
points: Security: HTTPS, CORS, CSP, rate limiting, SQL injection prevention
Scalability: Database indexing, caching, horizontal scaling support
Error handling: Centralized logging, error tracking, graceful failures
Monitoring: Health checks, performance metrics, uptime tracking
Testing: Unit tests, integration tests, E2E tests
Documentation: Code comments, API docs, deployment guides
CI/CD: Automated builds, tests, and deployments
Environment management: Dev, staging, production configs
Database migrations: Version control for schema changes
Backup and recovery: Automated backups, disaster recovery plans

benefits: Security best practices baked in (helmet.js, CORS, rate limiting)
Scalable architecture with database optimization
Error logging and monitoring hooks ready
Docker and CI/CD configs included
Deployed and tested in real SaaS applications

meta.title: Scalable SaaS Architecture | Built for Growth
meta.description: Handle 10 users or 10,000. Database optimization, caching, load balancing ready. Scalable architecture built into SassyPack.

hero.problem: Poor architecture kills growth. Slow queries, memory leaks, and single-server bottlenecks appear when you hit 100 users. Refactoring under load is a nightmare.

whyHard.heading: Scaling Challenges You'll Face
points: Database queries slow down as data grows (need indexes, query optimization)
Memory usage spikes with concurrent users (need connection pooling)
Single server can't handle traffic spikes (need load balancing)
Session management breaks with multiple servers (need Redis)
File uploads consume disk space (need cloud storage)
Background jobs block requests (need queue system)
Real-time features drain resources (need WebSocket management)
API rate limits needed to prevent abuse
Caching strategy required for repeated queries
Monitoring needed to catch bottlenecks early

solution.heading: The SassyPack Solution
solution.description: Architecture designed to scale from day one.
benefits: Database indexes and query optimization built-in
Connection pooling to handle concurrent users
Stateless design for horizontal scaling
Redis-ready for session management and caching
Cloud storage integration for file uploads

features.title: Optimized Database
